body.title: MILESTONE — Delta's pre-inference compiler measured at 51% token reduction (build_packet.v1)
body.source: Akien shared from Delta day-job (NOT mirrored into UU; informs it). Information only.
body.headline: First empirical proof that helper-first / pre-inference packaging pays for itself in tokens. Real isolated run: baseline 709 -> helper-first 347 tokens. Reduction 362 tokens / 51.06%. Status: improved. One step complete and tested in isolation.
body.what_it_is: build_packet.json (schema build.packet.v1, stage pre-inference): a DETERMINISTIC artifact that packages intent, constraints, and context BEFORE the LLM, so the model gets a cleaner input and is reserved for generation quality only.
body.why_milestone: The compiled-inference north star (move deterministic work out of inference; intent-driven build without LLM restart) rested on a BET that pre-inference packaging is net token-positive. This measures the bet at half. Bet -> data.
packet_shape_mirrors_uu_workflow_stack: context_shortlist (scored, with reasons) -> Librarian / symbols-table retrieval, deterministic
sufficiency_gate (pass/fail + missing_fields) -> CP1 'I don't know' made STRUCTURAL; refuses handoff when fields thin; cheapest piece to port (pure field-presence logic, no model call)
proof_plan.token_measurement -> proof-on-close as a first-class output; per-packet baseline-vs-helper-first count; compilation-depth metric accretes automatically, one packet at a time
consequence_check (risk_level, recommended_action) -> consequence-check ticket promoted to a packet field
determinism.fingerprint_sha256 -> same input -> same packet; what makes it a COMPILER not a prompt
body.convergence: Delta split deterministic-helpers vs LLM-generation; UU split zero-inference uu-CLI views vs reasoning skills (D-skills-two-products, Friday 2026-06-26). Same architecture, two directions, same week. The uu CLI is UU's first beachhead of the same principle.
body.direction_of_travel: UU's reasoning-bearing workflow skills are progressive-compilation candidates -> packet-builders that do deterministically what CC now does by hand. sufficiency_gate first (cheapest, most aligned with proof-on-close).
body.disposition: Information only per Akien. No UU ticket filed. Recorded as milestone for memory.

stale_flags.reason: TITLE IS WRONG AND IT MISLEADS. This note says 'measured at 51% token reduction'. 51% was never a MEASUREMENT — it was an ESTIMATE, and only for the ORIENTATION STEP ALONE (709->347 projection). The real figure is the WHOLE-BUILD, ACTUALLY MEASURED before/after: 89.19% (1675 -> 181 tokens, 1494 saved; artifact token.compare.generated.json), Copilot generating a test case from an intention-only description at Delta. Akien cites it as ~89.3%. THE WHOLE-BUILD MEASUREMENT BEAT THE SINGLE-STEP ESTIMATE — compounding across every build step is bigger than the orientation slice suggested. THIS NOTE ALREADY MISLED A SESSION: on 2026-07-12 CC.0 cited 51% back to Akien as the measured savings while arguing the economics of the compiled-inference thesis, and had to be corrected — despite holding the correct 89.19% figure in its own memory. A stale headline beat a correct memory. Do not cite 51% as a measurement. Do not compare either number to the build_packet CLI's 98.64%, which is a within-artifact ORIENTATION-SLICE ratio, not end-to-end.
